servers.description: Spring Voyage API (development default; override via adapter BaseUrl)

post.summary: Create a new API token

get.summary: List all API tokens for the current user

delete.summary: Revoke an API token by name

get.summary: Get the current authenticated user's profile
Get platform version, build hash, and license metadata
List every tenant in the platform registry

post.summary: Create a new tenant record

get.summary: Get a single tenant record by id

patch.summary: Update a tenant record's mutable fields (display name)

delete.summary: Soft-delete a tenant record

get.summary: List all registered agents

post.summary: Create a new agent

get.summary: Get agent status by sending a StatusQuery message

patch.summary: Update the agent's metadata (model, specialty, enabled, execution mode, instructions)

delete.summary: Delete an agent

get.summary: List the skill bundles equipped on an agent
get.description: Returns the resolved bundles in declaration order. Each entry carries the package + skill coordinates, a prompt-body summary, and the bundle's required-tool list.

post.summary: Equip a skill bundle on an agent
post.description: Idempotent on (packageName, skillName). The store re-resolves the bundle so the persisted record carries the freshest prompt + required-tools snapshot. Returns the new effective list in declaration order.

delete.summary: Unequip a skill bundle from an agent
delete.description: No-op when the bundle is not currently equipped. Returns the new effective list.

post.summary: Deploy (or reconcile) a persistent agent's backing container
Tear down a persistent agent's backing container (idempotent)
Start an agent (transitions Stopped → Starting → Running)
Stop a running agent (transitions Running → Stopping → Stopped)
Re-run the validation workflow for an agent (Draft/Error/Stopped → Validating). Settles in Stopped on success.
Adjust replica count for a persistent agent (OSS core supports 0 or 1 today)

get.summary: Read the container logs for a persistent agent
Get the current deployment state of a persistent agent (container + health)
Get the agent's runtime-status indicator (idle / busy / queued / unavailable)
Get the agent's persisted execution block

put.summary: Upsert one or more fields on the agent's execution block (partial update)

delete.summary: Clear the agent's execution block (strip all fields)

get.summary: List all registered units

post.summary: Create a new unit

get.summary: Get unit details and members

patch.summary: Update mutable unit metadata (displayName, description, model, color, hosting, instructions)

delete.summary: Delete a unit

get.summary: Check whether a unit is ready to leave Draft and be started
get.description: Returns readiness status and a list of missing requirements. Useful for the UI to enable/disable the Start button.

get.summary: Get the deployment / lifecycle status for a unit
get.description: Returns whether the unit is running and its current status label. Mirrors the agent deployment surface so the portal's Deployment tab renders start/stop controls for units and agents identically.

get.summary: List the skill bundles equipped on a unit
get.description: Returns the resolved bundles in declaration order. Each entry carries the package + skill coordinates, a prompt-body summary, and the bundle's required-tool list.

post.summary: Equip a skill bundle on a unit
post.description: Idempotent on (packageName, skillName). The store re-resolves the bundle so the persisted record carries the freshest prompt + required-tools snapshot. Returns the new effective list in declaration order.

delete.summary: Unequip a skill bundle from a unit
delete.description: No-op when the bundle is not currently equipped. Returns the new effective list.

post.summary: Start the runtime container for a unit
Stop the runtime container for a unit
Re-run backend validation for a unit in Error or Stopped state
post.description: Transitions the unit into Validating and kicks off a new ArtefactValidationWorkflow run. The handler returns immediately — progress is observable via SSE ValidationProgress events and the terminal state is written back by the workflow.

get.summary: List all members of a unit (agents and sub-units)
get.description: Returns the full member list from the unit actor, including both agent-scheme and unit-scheme members.

post.summary: Add a member to a unit

delete.summary: Remove a member from a unit

get.summary: Get a pointer to the unit's active connector binding, or 404 if unbound

delete.summary: Unbind the unit from whatever connector it was wired to

patch.summary: Set permission level for a human within a unit

delete.summary: Remove a human's permission entry from a unit

get.summary: Get all human permissions for a unit
List the agents that belong to this unit (members with scheme=agent), enriched with each agent's metadata

post.summary: Assign an agent to this unit. Creates a membership row (M:N per #160) and adds the agent to the unit's members list; no conflict is raised if the agent is also a member of another unit.

delete.summary: Unassign an agent from this unit. Deletes the membership row and removes the agent from the unit's members list; other memberships the agent holds are unaffected.

get.summary: Get the unit's runtime-status indicator (idle / busy / queued / unavailable)
List every team-role membership row attached to this unit.
get.description: Returns the rows from `unit_memberships_humans` for the unit in stable (created_at, id) order. Mirror of `sv.directory.list_members`'s human entries. Viewer-gated.

post.summary: Add a human as a unit team-role member.
post.description: Idempotent on the natural key (unit, human) — re-posting the same tuple updates roles + expertise + notifications in place rather than returning 409. Owner-gated.

patch.summary: Update roles / expertise / notifications on an existing team-role membership row.
patch.description: Multi-valued fields use replace semantics — omitted properties are treated as empty lists. Owner-gated; 404 when no row matches.

delete.summary: Remove a team-role membership row.
delete.description: Idempotent — returns 204 whether or not a matching row existed. Owner-gated.

get.summary: List sub-unit member rows on this unit with their per-membership roles + expertise.
get.description: Returns one entry per child sub-unit of this unit with the membership row's `roles` / `expertise` jsonb projections (#2463 / ADR-0046 §8 extended to sub-units). Viewer-gated.

patch.summary: Update roles + expertise on an existing agent-member row of this unit.
patch.description: Multi-valued fields use the standard tri-state semantics: null leaves the existing list untouched, an explicit empty array clears, a non-null array replaces. Owner-gated; 404 when no membership row matches the (unit, agent) natural key. Does not modify model / specialty / enabled / executionMode — those flow through `/memberships/{agentAddress}`.

patch.summary: Update roles + expertise on an existing sub-unit-member row of this unit.
patch.description: Multi-valued fields use the standard tri-state semantics: null leaves the existing list untouched, an explicit empty array clears, a non-null array replaces. Owner-gated; 404 when no parent → child sub-unit edge matches the natural key.

post.summary: Mint a new Human (Hat) row outside of the package-install path.
post.description: Allocates a fresh Guid, sets the explicit `tenantUserId` (or resolves the deployment default via ITenantUserDefaultResolver) and persists the row. DisplayName is validated via DisplayNameProblems.ValidateOrProblem. Returns the post-write HumanResponse so callers can render the row without a follow-up GET.

get.summary: Read a single human's read-side envelope (display name, description, email, platform role, created-at).
get.description: Returns the canonical fields needed by the Explorer Human page. Identity / membership lists live on dedicated sub-resources and are NOT embedded here.

patch.summary: Update a human's editable identity fields (display name, description).
patch.description: Omitted fields leave the existing value untouched (PATCH semantics). DisplayName is validated via DisplayNameProblems.ValidateOrProblem; description has no length limit. Returns the post-write HumanResponse.

delete.summary: Delete a human and cascade-remove every unit-membership row and ACL grant.
delete.description: Removes the human's row from `humans`, every `unit_membership_humans` row that names this human (so the human disappears from every parent unit's members), and every `unit_human_permissions` row (so stale grants do not survive the delete). Returns 204 on success, 404 when the id is not found in the current tenant, 400 when the id is empty.

patch.summary: Rebind a Human row to a different TenantUser (ADR-0062 § 1).
patch.description: Sets `humans.tenant_user_id = <body.tenantUserId>` and clears the old TenantUser's PrimaryHumanId pin when it pointed at this Human (so the operator does not silently retain a default Hat they no longer own). The replacement TenantUser must exist in the current tenant; an unknown id returns 404. Returns the post-write HumanResponse.

get.summary: Read a single tenant user's read-side envelope.
get.description: Returns the canonical fields needed by the portal's user-identity page and the CLI's read verbs. Identity lists live on the dedicated /identities sub-resource and are NOT embedded here.

patch.summary: Update a tenant user's editable identity fields (display name, description).
patch.description: Omitted fields leave the existing value untouched (PATCH semantics). DisplayName is validated via DisplayNameProblems.ValidateOrProblem; description has no length limit. Returns the post-write TenantUserResponse.

get.summary: Find a tenant user by OAuth subject within the current tenant (ADR-0062 § 6).
get.description: Returns the TenantUser whose auth_subject matches the supplied query parameter in the current tenant, or 404 when no row matches. The query parameter is required; an empty value surfaces as 400. Used by the CLI to resolve `<tenant-user-ref>` shapes typed as OAuth subjects (#2827).

post.summary: Create or update a tenant-user ↔ connector display-identity mapping (ADR-0047 §2).

get.summary: List every connector identity row mapped to this tenant user.

delete.summary: Remove a connector identity mapping by (tenantUser, connector, username). Idempotent — returns 204 even when nothing matched.

patch.summary: Pin the tenant user's primary Human (the default 'speaking-as' Hat for new outbound messages).
patch.description: ADR-0062 § 2: writes `tenant_users.primary_human_id`. The supplied Human must be bound to the target TenantUser via `humans.tenant_user_id`; an unbound Human returns 400 with a CLI-friendly message. Returns the post-write (tenantUserId, primaryHumanId) tuple.

get.summary: List the calling caller's bound Humans (Hats) with per-unit context (ADR-0062 §§ 3, 5).
get.description: Returns every Human row whose tenant_user_id FK points at the authenticated caller, with the IsPrimary flag set on the row that matches TenantUser.PrimaryHumanId. Each row carries a Memberships sub-list — one entry per UnitMembershipHuman row the Human appears on — so the portal's from-selector can render the per-Hat context label (e.g. designer in Magazine) in one round-trip.

get.summary: Get the unit's governance policy

put.summary: Upsert the unit's governance policy

get.summary: List every unit this agent belongs to, with per-membership config overrides
List every agent that is a member of this unit, with per-membership config overrides

put.summary: Create or update the per-membership config overrides for an agent in this unit

delete.summary: Remove an agent's membership of this unit

get.summary: List installed packages with per-package content counts
List unit templates discovered in the packages tree
Returns the raw YAML for a unit template inside a package
Returns detailed contents of a single package
Per-(provider, authMethod) credentials the package's units consume; pre-empts CredentialsMissing on install.
Returns detailed contents of a specific package version (ADR-0037 D5)

post.summary: Export an installed package back to its original package.yaml manifest. Supply either unitName or installId (not both). Use withValues=true to materialise resolved input values; secret inputs are emitted as placeholder references, never as cleartext.
Install one or more packages as a single atomic batch
post.description: Phase 1 (single EF transaction): validate, topo-sort, write staging rows. Phase 2 (post-commit): activate actors in dependency order. Returns 201 with the install status. Phase-2 failures appear as status=failed in the body; use GET /api/v1/installs/{id} for detail.

post.summary: Install a package from an uploaded YAML file (browse path, ADR-0035 decision 13)
post.description: Accepts a multipart/form-data upload containing the package YAML. For v0.1 the upload is one-shot: install and discard (no persistent tenant-scoped catalog). Same response shape as InstallPackages.

get.summary: Get install status, including per-package detail

post.summary: Re-run Phase 2 for a failed install
post.description: Re-activates every package whose state is not yet active. Phase 1 rows stay intact. Returns 200 with the updated status.

post.summary: Discard all staging rows for a failed install
post.description: Deletes every row in package_installs, unit_definitions, connector_definitions, and tenant_skill_bundle_bindings for this install_id. Runs in a single EF transaction. After abort the install is gone.

post.summary: Send a message routed via the message router

get.summary: Get a single message (envelope + body) by id
List all directory entries
Find directory entries by role

post.summary: Search the expertise directory (#542)
post.description: Free-text + structured search over the expertise directory. Outside a unit boundary, only projected entries are returned; inside, callers see the full aggregated scope. Step 1 is lexical / full-text — semantic search is tracked as a follow-up.

get.summary: Get an agent's configured expertise domains

put.summary: Replace an agent's configured expertise domains

get.summary: Get a unit's own (non-aggregated) expertise domains

put.summary: Replace a unit's own (non-aggregated) expertise domains

get.summary: Get the effective (recursive-aggregated) expertise of a unit
get.description: Walks the unit's member graph to the leaves, returning every capability contributed by the unit or any descendant, annotated with the contributing origin and the path from this unit to it.

get.summary: Get the unit's boundary configuration

put.summary: Upsert the unit's boundary configuration

delete.summary: Clear every boundary rule on the unit

get.summary: Get the unit's persisted execution defaults

put.summary: Upsert one or more fields on the unit's execution defaults (partial update)

delete.summary: Clear the unit's execution defaults (strip the block)

post.summary: Create a clone of an agent

get.summary: List clones of an agent
Get clone status

delete.summary: Clear the tenant-wide persistent cloning policy

get.summary: Get cost summary for an agent
Get per-model cost breakdown for an agent
Get cost summary for a unit
Get cost summary for the tenant

Query activity events with filters and pagination
Stream activity events via SSE
Get the tenant's activity-capture settings (level + retention).
Get the most recent external-forward attempt result for the tenant (#2503).
List threads derived from the activity event stream
Get a single thread (summary + ordered events)

post.summary: Record that the current human has read the specified inbox thread

get.summary: List every conversation thread in the tenant (tenant-wide observation)
Get a single observed thread (summary + ordered events) by id
Get the tenant-wide interactions graph (nodes / edges / timeline) for a time window
Stream live interactions via SSE (pulse / node-added / edge-added / throttled frames)
Get the tenant-wide interactions history (nodes / edges / per-message pulses) for a time window
Get throughput counters (messages / turns / tool calls) per source over a time range
Get wait-time rollups per source; durations are computed from paired StateChanged lifecycle transitions
Get a zero-filled cost time-series for an agent, suitable for sparkline rendering
Get a zero-filled cost time-series for a unit, suitable for sparkline rendering
Get an aggregated dashboard summary with unit/agent counts, status breakdown, recent activity, and total cost
Get a summary of all registered agents
Get a summary of all registered units
Get aggregated cost data
Synthesized tenant → units → agents tree with multi-parent alias edges
Read the unit's short-term and long-term memory entries (#2342)
Read the agent's short-term and long-term memory entries (#2342)
Read a single memory entry by id, scoped to the unit (#2342)
Read a single memory entry by id, scoped to the agent (#2342)
Open operational issues against a unit, plus the transitively-aggregated descendant rollup.
Open operational issues against an agent. Agents have no descendants — the rollup is always empty.
Batch open-issue counts for many subjects in one round-trip.
List every tool exposed by every registered skill registry

delete.summary: Deprovision a connector type platform-wide (PlatformOperator only)

Get the JSON Schema describing the GitHub connector per-TenantUser display-identity body

List every model provider installed on the current tenant

Get the current credential-health row for a provider on the current tenant

post.summary: Probe the provider with the supplied credential; record the outcome in the credential-health store; does not touch the model catalogue
Best-effort live-catalogue lookup; replaces the tenant's configured model list on success

get.summary: List secret metadata for a unit. Never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Register a unit-scoped secret. Provide exactly one of 'value' (pass-through write) or 'externalStoreKey' (bind existing reference).

put.summary: Rotate a unit-scoped secret by appending a new version. Returns the new version number. Prior versions remain resolvable via the pin overload until pruned.

delete.summary: Delete a unit-scoped secret (all versions). Underlying plaintext is removed only for platform-owned versions; external references leave the external store key untouched.

get.summary: List retained versions for a unit-scoped secret. Metadata only; never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Prune older versions of a unit-scoped secret, retaining the N most-recent. 'keep' must be >= 1; the current version is always retained.

get.summary: List secret metadata for an agent. Never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Register an agent-scoped secret. Provide exactly one of 'value' or 'externalStoreKey'. The 'propagate' flag has no effect at agent scope (agents have no descendants).

put.summary: Rotate an agent-scoped secret by appending a new version. Returns the new version number.

delete.summary: Delete an agent-scoped secret (all versions). Underlying plaintext is removed only for platform-owned versions; external references leave the external store key untouched.

get.summary: List retained versions for an agent-scoped secret. Metadata only; never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Prune older versions of an agent-scoped secret, retaining the N most-recent. 'keep' must be >= 1; the current version is always retained.

get.summary: List secret metadata for the current tenant. Never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Register a tenant-scoped secret. Provide exactly one of 'value' (pass-through write) or 'externalStoreKey' (bind existing reference).

put.summary: Rotate a tenant-scoped secret by appending a new version. Returns the new version number.

delete.summary: Delete a tenant-scoped secret (all versions). Underlying plaintext is removed only for platform-owned versions; external references leave the external store key untouched.

get.summary: List retained versions for a tenant-scoped secret. Metadata only; never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Prune older versions of a tenant-scoped secret, retaining the N most-recent. 'keep' must be >= 1.

get.summary: List platform-scoped secret metadata. Never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Register a platform-scoped secret. Provide exactly one of 'value' (pass-through write) or 'externalStoreKey' (bind existing reference).

put.summary: Rotate a platform-scoped secret by appending a new version. Returns the new version number.

delete.summary: Delete a platform-scoped secret (all versions). Underlying plaintext is removed only for platform-owned versions; external references leave the external store key untouched.

get.summary: List retained versions for a platform-scoped secret. Metadata only; never returns plaintext or store keys.

post.summary: Prune older versions of a platform-scoped secret, retaining the N most-recent. 'keep' must be >= 1.

get.summary: Report whether an LLM provider credential / endpoint is configured for the requested auth method
Return the cached startup configuration validation report

post.summary: Receive a GitHub webhook event
